description_long: This bungalow is a single-story, L-shaped residential building with a light beige exterior and a dark, weathered gable roof. The structure features multiple rectangular windows arranged in a regular pattern along each facade, providing ample natural light to the interior. The front entrance is a simple, recessed doorway without a porch, and the building's siding appears to be horizontal paneling. The roof is pitched and extends over both wings of the house, forming a cross-gabled configuration. One side of the building has a larger window, possibly for a living area, while the rest of the windows are smaller and evenly spaced. The overall design is functional and modest, typical of suburban or rural neighborhoods.
description: A light beige, L-shaped bungalow with a dark cross-gabled roof and regularly spaced rectangular windows. The design is simple, with a recessed front entrance and no porch.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance centered on the shorter wing, flanked by two windows on each side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the recessed doorway on the longer wing, with three windows on each side and no porch.
description_view_2: The side view reveals a large window on the left wing and a secondary entrance on the right, with the cross-gabled roof visible.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a symmetrical arrangement of six windows, three on each wing, and no visible entrances.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front and rear windows with a large side window
